THIS IS AN IMPROPER MOTION. THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT DOES NOT SET CALENDAR DATES FOR A PARTY'S MOTIONS. IT IS UP TO THE PARTY TO NOTICE A MOTION FOR A PARTICULAR TIME AND DAY. IF A PARTY THEN SEEKS TO PRESENT ORAL TESTIMONY, A PARTY MAY MAKE THE REQUEST PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1306. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND FOR ORDER STRIKING THE FIRST-AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Charles M. Collins, Esq. 355-0310 for pltf & opposing party; Ray Bronner, 650/619-1884 deft & moving party in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. IT IS A RECOGNIZED PLEADING METHOD TO INCORPORATE ALLEGATIONS BY REFERENCE. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS DID NOT NEED TO BE RAISED IN THE LOCKS CASE BECAUSE THE LOCKS CASE INVOLVED A DIFFERENT TRANSACTION AND PLAINTIFF NEVER FILED AN ANSWER. SEE CCP SECTION 426.30(b)(2). DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS UNTIMELY. CCP SECTION 435(b)(1). THE BACK RENT CLAIM COULD NOT BE RAISED IN THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE THAT WAS BASED ON NUISANCE. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF & HIS COUNSEL OF RECORD.

APPEARANCES: Charles M. Collins, Esq. 355-0310 for pltf & opposing party; Ray Bronner, 650/619-1884 deft & moving party in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY DEFECTIVE. DEFENDANT DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE SAFE HARBOR PROVISION SET FORTH IN CCP SECTION 128.7(c)(1). DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THIS ACTION IS "FRIVOLOUS" WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP SECTION 128.7. PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. In addition, plaintiff's request for attorney's fees denied, however Court would consider any proper motion brought before the Court. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Defendant Ray Bronner'S Motion For Calendaring Hearing On Sanctions (Ccp 128, 12.5, 128.6, 128.7)
Off Calendar Jan-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant Ray Bronner's Motion For Calendaring Hearing On Sanctions [C.C.P. Sections 128, 12.5, 128.6, 128.7].
APPEARANCES: Ray Bronner, 650/619-1884 deft & moving party in propria persona (indicated that he had given notice to the other side at the last hearing that he would contest tentative ruling); no other appearances.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. THIS IS AN IMPROPER MOTION. THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT DOES NOT SET CALENDAR DATES FOR A PARTY'S MOTIONS. IT IS UP TO THE PARTY TO NOTICE A MOTION FOR A PARTICULAR TIME AND DAY. IF A PARTY THEN SEEKS TO PRESENT ORAL TESTIMONY, A PARTY MAY MAKE THE REQUEST PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1306. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

APPEARANCES: Charles M. Collins, Esq. 355-0310 for pltf & opposing party; Ray Bronner, 650/619-1884 deft & moving party in propria persona.
RULING:
Argued and the Court ruled as follows: Court does not rule on the 5th cause of action based on plaintiff's filing of a dismissal. The Court adopted its remaining tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ESTABLISH PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFF SHIFTS THE BURDEN UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16. DEFENDANTS EVIDENCE FAILS TO OVERCOME THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE THAT PROTECTS ALL FORMS OF CONDUCT THAT OCCUR IN LITIGATION AS LONG AS THE CONDUCT FURTHERS A PARTIES LITIGATION OBJECTIVE. SILBERG V. ANDERSON (1990) 50 CAL.3D 205, 212; NAVEILIER V. SLETTEN (2003) 106 CAL.APP. 4TH 763, 771. Court will prepare an order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
